english_explanations: To go to someone or something for help, advice, or support.
example_sentences: When times are tough, people often turn to family for support.

english_explanations: Over an extended period of time; ultimately.
example_sentences: Investing in education pays off in the long run.

english_explanations: To work together cooperatively, especially in a difficult situation.
example_sentences: The community had to pull together to rebuild after the disaster.

english_explanations: To have enough money, time, or ability to do something without negative consequences.
example_sentences: Many young people cannot afford to buy a house in major cities.

word: bring a relationship to its knees
english_explanations: To cause a relationship to nearly collapse or fail completely.
example_sentences: Financial stress can bring a relationship to its knees.

english_explanations: To describe the distinctive nature or features of something.
example_sentences: The era was characterized by rapid technological advancement.

english_explanations: To be burdened with something undesirable, such as debt or responsibility.
example_sentences: The company was saddled with enormous debt after the acquisition.

english_explanations: To succeed in doing something or to arrive somewhere successfully.
example_sentences: Despite the challenges, she managed to make it to the top of her field.

english_explanations: To cause something to progress much more slowly or with greater difficulty.
example_sentences: High inflation put a huge drag on economic growth last year.

english_explanations: To fully repay a debt or loan.
example_sentences: It took him five years to pay off his student loans.

english_explanations: To escape from a difficult situation, especially debt or obligation.
example_sentences: After years of struggle, she finally got out from under her credit card debt.

english_explanations: To give up one thing in order to receive another.
example_sentences: Many people trade time for money without considering the long-term cost.

english_explanations: To obtain or acquire something, often used in negative or interrogative contexts.
example_sentences: Good jobs are hard to come by in this competitive market.

english_explanations: A system or arrangement in which neither party is considered responsible for a problem or failure.
example_sentences: No-fault divorce laws have made it easier for couples to separate amicably.

english_explanations: To reduce the effect of something by having an opposite effect of equal force.
example_sentences: The gains in one sector were cancelling out the losses in another.

english_explanations: To believe in or accept an idea, or to purchase a share of something.
example_sentences: Not everyone buys into the idea that money brings happiness.

english_explanations: To be in a situation where one must be very careful to avoid making a mistake.
example_sentences: Managers often walk a fine line between being approachable and maintaining authority.

english_explanations: Describing a person who rises from poverty to great wealth or success.
example_sentences: The entrepreneur's rags-to-riches story inspired millions around the world.

english_explanations: At or reaching an extremely high level.
example_sentences: Housing prices in the city have gone sky-high in recent years.

english_explanations: Used to indicate the opposite or a different choice from what was previously mentioned.
example_sentences: Rather than giving up, she worked even harder to achieve her goals.

english_explanations: Feeling unable to cope with the amount or intensity of something.
example_sentences: She felt overwhelmed by the demands of her new job.

english_explanations: In a way that cannot be fixed or made right again.
example_sentences: The damage to their friendship was done irreparably.

english_explanations: To serve as a symbol of something; to represent a deeper meaning.
example_sentences: The dove symbolizes peace in many cultures.

english_explanations: A social or cultural custom forbidding or restricting a particular practice.
example_sentences: Talking about salary is still considered a taboo in some workplaces.

english_explanations: Gradually increasing in quantity over time.
example_sentences: He spent years accumulating wealth through smart investments.

english_explanations: To continue to exist or occur over a prolonged period.
example_sentences: Despite efforts to reduce it, poverty continues to persist in the region.

english_explanations: The result or outcome of an effort, often a positive one; also the act of paying a debt.
example_sentences: The payoff for years of hard work was finally a promotion.

english_explanations: A person or thing that corresponds to or has the same function as another in a different place or organization.
example_sentences: She met with her counterparts from the international offices.

english_explanations: The rate at which employees leave and are replaced, or the total amount of business done in a period.
example_sentences: High turnover can be a sign of poor management in a company.

english_explanations: To cause a plan or process to fail or go off track.
example_sentences: Unexpected costs are derailing the project's timeline.

english_explanations: A woman who is believed to predict the future or speak for a divine power.
example_sentences: In ancient times, a prophetess was often consulted before major decisions.

english_explanations: At the most basic level; in an essential way.
example_sentences: The two approaches are fundamentally different in their philosophy.

english_explanations: Lacking optimism or hope; cold and unwelcoming in appearance.
example_sentences: The economic forecast looks bleak for the coming quarter.

english_explanations: The horizontal bars on a ladder; metaphorically, levels in a hierarchy or career progression.
example_sentences: She climbed the rungs of the corporate ladder with determination.

english_explanations: A person who seeks to promote the welfare of others, especially through charitable donations.
example_sentences: The philanthropist donated millions to educational causes.

english_explanations: A wealthy, powerful person in business or industry.
example_sentences: The media tycoon built an empire spanning multiple continents.

english_explanations: The act of cutting away unnecessary parts; reducing costs or excess.
example_sentences: The company is trimming its workforce to cut costs.
